source: VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (GDXJ) holdings — restricted to US-primary-listed precious metals miners and royalty companies that yfinance can fetch reliably for the 2010-2024 backtest window. The published GDXJ basket includes many TSX/ASX-primary names which are excluded here to avoid yfinance suffix-resolution flakiness.
survivorship_warning: Frozen 2026 snapshot of US-listed junior/mid-tier precious metals miners. Survivorship bias is high — second only to XBI in this repo. Junior mining is brutally selection-biased: most pre-2010 explorers and developers either failed (no deposit, ran out of capital, hit a permitting wall), got acquired, or merged. Names like Pretium, SilverCrest, Yamana, Goldcorp are missing because they're gone. The flip side: gold/silver miners are highly idiosyncratic relative to broad equity factors — pricing is dominated by gold-price beta, drilling-result events, permitting decisions, and capital-raise overhangs. Like biotech, this is a flow-pressure-rich, retail-heavy basket with frequent forced selling on bad news; reversal-style edges are mechanistically plausible. Also note: a subset are royalty/streaming companies (FNV, RGLD, WPM, SAND, OR, EMX, GROY) whose business model is fundamentally different from mining — they trade on a hybrid royalty+miner factor, lower vol than operators, and their inclusion is faithful to GDXJ but blurs the 'junior miner' label. Documented, not fixed.
